chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Date: Order with signature(s) of the Judge(s) ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- CP.No.D-8706 of 2017 For hearing of CMA No. 22526/2021. (Contempt) CP.No.D-6640 of 2017 For hearing of CMA No. 22524/2021. (Contempt) ------------------- 29th September 2021 Mr. Arshad Khan Tanoli, advocate for the petitioners. Mr. Muhammad Nishat Warsi, DAG. Mr. Amir Latif, advocate for NBP (Respondents No. 1 to 3). ------------------ Learned counsel for the respondents No. 1 to 3 files compliance report, which is as under:- “ Compliance report /Statement. By and on behalf of the respondents No. 1 to 3 (M/S. National Bank of Pakistan), it is respectfully submitted as under:- 1- That the petitioners who have not opted for compromise/agreement/discharge in favor of NBP are covered by the MTO Scheme and are alike/identical to the respondents in C.A. No. 1644/2013 have been extended the same benefits as per judgment dated 21.09.2016 passed by the Apex Court in C.A. No. 1644/2013. 2- It is further submitted that the arrears are being paid in 03 installments instead of 06 installments as directed by the Hon’ble Supreme Court in the judgment referred hereinabove. 3- In addition to the above, it is further submitted that the final installment shall be made with the salary of December, 2021. 4- It is also submitted that if any petitioner feels any grievance in respect of his fixation may make representation to the competent authority of the Bank through proper channel for the rederassal thereof. 5- In view of above statement this contempt application may be disposed of.

No. 1644/2013 have been extended the same benefits as per judgment dated 21.09.2016 passed by the Apex Court in C.A. No. 1644/2013. 2- It is further submitted that the arrears are being paid in 03 installments instead of 06 installments as directed by the Hon’ble Supreme Court in the judgment referred hereinabove. 3- In addition to the above, it is further submitted that the final installment shall be made with the salary of December, 2021. 4- It is also submitted that if any petitioner feels any grievance in respect of his fixation may make representation to the competent authority of the Bank through proper channel for the rederassal thereof. 5- In view of above statement this contempt application may be disposed of. Karachi. Sd/- Dated: 29.09.2021 Advocate for Respondents No. 1 to 3.” - { 2 } - Accordingly, in view of above, learned counsel for the petitioners seeks disposal of contempt application, hence, the same is disposed of. J U D G E J U D G E SAJID.
